question.text: Nifty is at 18,000. A call with strike 17,500 has premium Rs. 600 and a call with strike 18,500 has premium Rs. 120. Which has higher time value?

answer.text: The 18,500 strike call has higher time value (Rs. 120 vs Rs. 100)
The 17,500 strike call has higher time value (Rs. 600 vs Rs. 120)
Cannot be determined without knowing the expiry date

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** 17,500 strike call (ITM): - IV = max(18,000 - 17,500, 0) = 500 - TV = 600 - 500 = Rs. 100
18,500 strike call (OTM): - IV = max(18,000 - 18,500, 0) = 0 - TV = 120 - 0 = Rs. 120
The OTM call has higher time value (120 > 100).
**The Trap:** Higher premium does NOT mean higher time value. Decompose into IV and TV.
**Cross-Exam Context:** ATM options generally have the highest time value of all strikes.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Compare time values, not total premiums. ATM options typically have maximum time value.
